text: Anyone seeing intermittent 502s from the API gateway in the last hour? Got two reports from users.

ack, will keep an eye on it

text: PR up for the search index refactor: <https://example.com/repo/pull/482>. Would love a second pair of eyes on the migration script in particular.

replies.text: Will review after standup

text: Quick reminder: do *not* run `make seed` against the staging DB without checking with me first — there's a known race that wipes the catalog table.

Got bitten by this gotcha today:
```
for x in queryset:
    x.delete()
```
This re-evaluates the queryset every iteration. Use `queryset.delete()` or iterate over a `list(queryset)` first.

replies.text: oof, classic. Worth adding to the team wiki?
Will do

text: Heads-up: bumping the lockfile to pull in the new `httpx` version. Run `pip install -r requirements.txt` after your next pull.
Postmortem doc for yesterday's incident is up. TL;DR: a single environment variable rename broke autoscaling. Two action items.

Debugging tip of the day: if you ever see
`asyncio.exceptions.CancelledError`
with no other context in a `aiohttp` server, check whether your handler is `async` but blocks on a sync call. Took me 2 hours.
What does the team think about adopting `ruff` for linting? It's basically a drop-in for flake8 + isort + bunch of others, and ~100x faster.

replies.text: +1, we've been wanting to consolidate the linter zoo anyway
Same. Want to open a PR with the initial config?
On it

text:
Anyone seen this before?
```
FATAL: too many connections for role "app_prod" (currently 100)
```
Happens once or twice a day, never long enough to alert.

replies.text: Sounds like a connection-pool leak on one of the workers. PgBouncer in front would mask this but the underlying leak is worth finding.
Yeah, I'll add some pool-metrics instrumentation

text: New deploy diagram is up in the docs folder — please yell if anything is wrong.
Anyone want to pair on the cache-invalidation work this afternoon? Could use a second brain.

replies.text: I'm free after 3
:handshake:

text: I'm going to start enforcing the *no merges before tests pass* rule in CI as a hard block, not just a warning. Heads up.
Reminder for newer folks: branch names follow `<initials>/<short-kebab>`. Helps with the auto-routing for code review.
Spec for the audit-log feature is open for review — comments welcome. Key open question is retention: 30d, 90d, or 1y?

replies.text: 90d unless we get a compliance requirement that pushes us to 1y. Cheaper and still useful for debugging.

text: Spent the morning chasing a memory leak that turned out to be... a missing `await` on a cleanup function. The classic.

replies.text: Linter rule for that exists in pyflakes, we should turn it on

text: Updated the local dev README with the new docker-compose flow. `make dev` should now Just Work :tm:
Architecture diagram for the new ingestion pipeline. Three stages, async between each, idempotent retries built in.

replies.text: Like the explicit dead-letter queue. One question: how do we replay from there?
Manual CLI command for now, will spec a self-service flow once we see how often it gets used

text: TIL: `pg_stat_statements` is way more useful than I gave it credit for. Found three queries we can index our way out of.
Tests are flaky on `test_retry_with_jitter` — anyone seen this? Looks like a timing assertion that's too tight.

replies.text: Yeah, I bumped the tolerance in PR #471 — should be in main already. Pull?
Ah missed that. Pulling now

text: Cleanup PR: removed 1.2k lines of dead code. Big chunk was the legacy v1 import path nobody's used in months.

replies.text: Thank you :pray:

text: Notes from this morning's architecture review: leaning toward event-driven for the notifications subsystem. Detailed write-up coming.
First successful end-to-end run of the new pipeline. ~3 minutes for the full backfill. We can probably squeeze more out of it but this is already a 5x improvement.
Joining the engineering channel for the first time as a new hire :wave: looking forward to digging in.
